plan: We will significantly reduce SPY to meet concentration limits and trim QQQ.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero for capital preservation.
QQQI, an income-focused ETF, will be increased to provide stability.
SMH will be trimmed to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
This plan aims for capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
A substantial cash buffer will be maintained for stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility and drawdown risk. Returns will be steadier but potentially lower than other modes.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to comply with concentration limits.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero, reducing overall leverage.
QQQ and SMH will be trimmed to reduce exposure to downtrending assets.
A new position in ZS will be initiated, leveraging a high-scoring setup.
This plan balances growth potential with a focus on risk management.
A moderate cash buffer will be held.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing a high-quality new name. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown potential, aiming for consistent growth.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
All current leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold due to being below stops.
QQQ and SMH will be trimmed to reduce exposure to downtrending assets.
New positions in high-scoring NVDA and ZS will be initiated for growth.
This plan leans into high-conviction setups for aggressive growth.
A small cash buffer will be maintained, using leverage for amplification.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It focuses on high-scoring setups and uses leverage to amplify potential returns. Be aware that this approach significantly increases both upside and drawdown potential, and leveraged ETFs carry daily decay risk.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to meet concentration limits and free capital for income.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero for capital preservation.
QQQI will be significantly increased to boost monthly distributions.
QQQ and CRWD will be trimmed to reduce growth and high-beta exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
A cash buffer will be maintained to manage volatility.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on reliable monthly distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta names, rotating into income-generating ETFs. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
